text.title: Engagement Strategy, Lead Generation, and Metrics
text.summary: Comment strategy for authority building, connection request personalization, DM welcome sequences, lead generation tactics, and the five metrics that indicate LinkedIn growth health.

rule.statement: Comment with substantive additions — a specific reaction, your experience or counter-data, and a question — never with "Great post!" or emoji-only responses.
rule.rationale: Substantive comments are indexed by LinkedIn and seen by the original poster's followers. Emoji-only comments are ignored by the algorithm and signal low-quality engagement to observers.

example.code: "This resonates. I tried X approach last year and found Y worked better for our audience. Have you tested that variation?"

rule.statement: Comment early (within the first hour) on posts from key people in your niche — early engagement receives disproportionate algorithm amplification.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's algorithm amplifies posts with rapid early engagement. Being among the first 5-10 commenters puts your comment at the top of the thread and in front of the poster's entire audience.

rule.statement: Personalize every connection request with a specific observation about the recipient's work — generic requests have a 20-30% acceptance rate vs 50%+ for personalized ones.
rule.rationale: Personalization signals that you have actually looked at the profile, which differentiates from the mass-connection pattern that most people recognize and reject.

rule.statement: Follow up new connections with a value-first DM, not a pitch — the first DM must give something (resource, insight, genuine compliment) before asking for anything.
rule.rationale: Pitching in the first DM is the single most common LinkedIn mistake; it triggers immediate disconnection and harms your reputation with that person.

example.note: DM welcome template for new connections.
example.code:
Hey [Name], thanks for connecting!
I noticed you're [something specific from their profile].
I share [content type] about [topic] here — hope you find it useful.
If you ever want to chat about [topic], always happy to connect.
[Your Name]

section.title: Lead Generation Tactics

rule.statement: Enable Creator Mode before any growth push — it adds a follower button and newsletter CTA that standard profiles lack.
rule.rationale: Creator Mode changes the primary action button from "Connect" to "Follow," which has lower friction for new visitors and directly grows your addressable audience.

list.item: Lead magnets: link to a free resource in the Featured section
Newsletter: CTA at end of every post drives subscribers
Content mentions: natural product references inside valuable content

section.reference: Parse Shield analytics CSV to find top posts by engagement rate

rule.statement: Track engagement rate (target 3%, great 7%+) and profile views per week (target 200, great 1K+) as the primary health signals — follower count is a lagging indicator.
rule.rationale: Follower count grows slowly and can be inflated by low-quality connections. Engagement rate and profile views indicate whether content and profile are converting attention into interest.

list.item: Profile views per week: good 200, great 1K+

antipattern.description: Posting corporate speak or company announcements as personal content.
antipattern.reason: Nobody follows a person to read press releases. LinkedIn rewards personal voice; corporate voice produces near-zero organic reach.

antipattern.description: Posting inconsistently — 3 posts one week, nothing for 2 weeks.
antipattern.reason: LinkedIn's algorithm weights recency and consistency. Gaps reset your distribution baseline and require rebuilding momentum from scratch.
